questions.question_text: How many total hours of continuing education must Maine associate brokers and designated brokers complete each renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Maine requires 21 hours of continuing education every 2-year renewal cycle for associate brokers and designated brokers. This includes 3 hours of mandatory core content and 18 hours of elective courses. Sales agents cannot renew their licenses.

questions.question_text: An associate broker plans her CE schedule. How many hours must come from the mandatory core course?
questions.explanation: Of the 21 total CE hours required, 3 hours must be the mandatory core course on a topic selected by MREC. The remaining 18 hours may be fulfilled through approved elective courses of the licensee's choosing.

questions.question_text: Who selects the mandatory core course topic for Maine real estate CE each renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: The Maine Real Estate Commission selects the mandatory core course topic each renewal cycle. The 3-hour core course covers a topic MREC deems important for current practice. The remaining 18 hours of the 21-hour CE requirement are elective.

questions.question_text: A sales agent approaching her 2-year license expiration asks what CE she needs to renew. What is the correct response?
questions.explanation: Maine sales agent licenses are non-renewable. CE requirements apply only to associate brokers and designated brokers. A sales agent must upgrade to associate broker before the license expires, or the license simply lapses.

questions.question_text: A designated broker completed 3 hours of core CE and 12 hours of electives before her renewal deadline. What is her status?
distractors: She has met the CE requirement
She must retake the core course
questions.explanation: The total CE requirement is 21 hours: 3 mandatory core plus 18 elective. She completed 3 + 12 = 15 hours, which leaves her 6 elective hours short. She must complete the remaining hours before renewal or face late penalties.

questions.question_text: Which license types are eligible for biennial renewal in Maine?
distractors: All license types including sales agent
questions.explanation: Only associate broker and designated broker licenses are renewable in Maine. Sales agent licenses are non-renewable 2-year licenses — they cannot be renewed under any circumstances. Sales agents must upgrade to associate broker to continue practicing.

questions.question_text: An associate broker misses her renewal deadline by 30 days. What must she do to restore her license?
questions.correct_answer: Renew within 90 days with a late fee
distractors: Reapply as a brand new licensee
questions.explanation: Maine allows a 90-day grace period after the renewal deadline. Since she is only 30 days late, she is still within the grace period and can renew by paying the $100 renewal fee plus a $50 late fee and completing any outstanding CE requirements.

questions.question_text: What happens if a Maine licensee renews after the deadline but within the grace period?
questions.correct_answer: A late fee is added to the renewal fee
distractors: No penalty applies during the grace period
The licensee must retake the state exam
questions.explanation: Licensees who renew during the 90-day grace period must pay a late fee in addition to the standard renewal fee. This penalty encourages timely renewal while still giving licensees a window to maintain their license without starting over.

questions.question_text: A sales agent's 2-year license is about to expire. She wants to continue practicing. What is her best course of action?
questions.explanation: Sales agent licenses in Maine are non-renewable. To continue practicing, the agent must meet associate broker requirements (60 additional education hours, 2 years active experience, field experience form) and upgrade before the license expires. Paying fees or completing CE alone will not renew a sales agent license.

questions.question_text: An associate broker lets her license lapse for 100 days past the renewal deadline without renewing. What is the consequence?
questions.correct_answer: She must reapply for a new license
distractors: She can still pay the late fee
questions.explanation: The 90-day grace period is the final opportunity to renew late. At 100 days past the deadline, the grace period has expired and the license is no longer eligible for late renewal. She must go through the full licensing process again as a new applicant.
